{"body":"### Preflight Checklist

- [x] I have searched [existing issues](https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/issues?q=is%3Aissue+label%3Abug) and this hasn't been reported yet
- [x] I am using the latest version of Moltis

### What happened?

Manual context compaction via **/compact** breaks further conversation in this chat.
llama.cpp ised shows following error: 
`Error rendering prompt with jinja template: "No user query found in messages.".`
Any further attempts to engage with model in the same chat gives the same error. Issue persists with forking the chat.

Current setup: Moltis running on rPi, LLMs served with llama.cpp over local network from PC.



### Expected behavior

Conversation continues w/o errors

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open new chat, write something to get a response
2. /compact
3. Try to continue chatting

### Did this happen during a chat session?

Yes

### Chat session context (if applicable)

```markdown

```

LLM side:

```shell

```

    59→/// Extract preview text from a single message JSON value.
    60→fn extract_preview_from_value(msg: &Value) -> Option<String> {
    61→    fn message_text(msg: &Value) -> Option<String> {
    62→        let text = if let Some(s) = msg.get("content").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {
    63→            s.to_string()
    64→        } else if let Some(blocks) = msg.get("content").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {
    65→            blocks
    66→                .iter()
    67→                .filter_map(|b| {
    68→                    if b.get("type").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) == Some("text") {
    69→                        b.get("text").and_then(|v| v.as_str())
    70→                    } else {
    71→                        None
    72→                    }
    73→                })
    74→                .collect::<Vec<_>>()
    75→                .join(" ")
    76→        } else {
    77→            return None;
    78→        };
    79→        let trimmed = text.trim();
    80→        if trimmed.is_empty() {
    81→            None
    82→        } else {
    83→            Some(trimmed.to_string())
    84→        }
    85→    }
    86→    fn truncate_preview(s: &str, max: usize) -> String {
    87→        if s.len() <= max {
    88→            s.to_string()
    89→        } else {
    90→            format!("{}…", &s[..s.floor_char_boundary(max)])
    91→        }
    92→    }
    93→    message_text(msg).map(|t| truncate_preview(&t, 200))
    94→}

   120→/// Convert session-crate `MessageContent` to agents-crate `UserContent`.
   121→///
   122→/// The two types have different image representations:
   123→/// - `ContentBlock::ImageUrl` stores a data URI string
   124→/// - `ContentPart::Image` stores separated `media_type` + `data` fields
   125→fn to_user_content(mc: &MessageContent) -> UserContent {
   126→    match mc {
   127→        MessageContent::Text(text) => UserContent::Text(text.clone()),
   128→        MessageContent::Multimodal(blocks) => {
   129→            let parts: Vec<ContentPart> = blocks
   130→                .iter()
   131→                .filter_map(|block| match block {
   132→                    ContentBlock::Text { text } => Some(ContentPart::Text(text.clone())),
   133→                    ContentBlock::ImageUrl { image_url } => match parse_data_uri(&image_url.url) {
   134→                        Some((media_type, data)) => {
   135→                            debug!(
   136→                                media_type,
   137→                                data_len = data.len(),
   138→                                "to_user_content: parsed image from data URI"
   139→                            );
   140→                            Some(ContentPart::Image {
   141→                                media_type: media_type.to_string(),
   142→                                data: data.to_string(),
   143→                            })
   144→                        },
   145→                        None => {
   146→                            warn!(
   147→                                url_prefix = truncate_at_char_boundary(&image_url.url, 80),
   148→                                "to_user_content: failed to parse data URI, dropping image"
   149→                            );
   150→                            None
   151→                        },
   152→                    },
   153→                })
   154→                .collect();
   155→            let text_count = parts
   156→                .iter()
   157→                .filter(|p| matches!(p, ContentPart::Text(_)))
   158→                .count();
   159→            let image_count = parts
   160→                .iter()
   161→                .filter(|p| matches!(p, ContentPart::Image { .. }))
   162→                .count();
   163→            debug!(
   164→                text_count,
   165→                image_count,
   166→                total_blocks = blocks.len(),
   167→                "to_user_content: converted multimodal content"
   168→            );
   169→            UserContent::Multimodal(parts)
   170→        },
   171→    }

1→//! Typed message structures for session storage.
     2→//!
     3→//! These types represent the JSON format stored in session JSONL files.
     4→//! They include both LLM-relevant fields (role, content) and metadata
     5→//! fields (created_at, model, provider, tokens, channel).
     6→
     7→use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
     8→
     9→/// A message stored in a session JSONL file.
    10→///
    11→/// Includes both the LLM-relevant content and metadata for UI display
    12→/// and analytics. The `role` field determines which variant this is.
    13→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    14→#[serde(tag = "role", rename_all = "lowercase")]
    15→pub enum PersistedMessage {
    16→    System {
    17→        content: String,
    18→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    19→        created_at: Option<u64>,
    20→    },
    21→    /// UI-only informational message (not part of LLM prompt history).
    22→    Notice {
    23→        content: String,
    24→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    25→        created_at: Option<u64>,
    26→    },
    27→    User {
    28→        /// Content can be a string (plain text) or array (multimodal).
    29→        content: MessageContent,
    30→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    31→        created_at: Option<u64>,
    32→        /// Relative media path for uploaded user audio (e.g. "media/main/voice-123.webm").
    33→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    34→        audio: Option<String>,
    35→        /// Channel metadata for UI display (e.g., Telegram sender info).
    36→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    37→        channel: Option<serde_json::Value>,
    38→        /// Client-assigned sequence number for ordering diagnostics.
    39→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    40→        seq: Option<u64>,
    41→        /// Agent run ID that processes this message (parent→child link).
    42→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    43→        run_id: Option<String>,
    44→    },
    45→    Assistant {
    46→        content: String,
    47→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    48→        created_at: Option<u64>,
    49→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    50→        model: Option<String>,
    51→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    52→        provider: Option<String>,
    53→        /// Total input tokens spent during this assistant turn.
    54→        #[serde(rename = "inputTokens", skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    55→        input_tokens: Option<u32>,
    56→        /// Total output tokens produced during this assistant turn.
    57→        #[serde(rename = "outputTokens", skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    58→        output_tokens: Option<u32>,
    59→        #[serde(rename = "durationMs", skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    60→        duration_ms: Option<u64>,
    61→        /// Input tokens sent in the final LLM request for this turn.
    62→        #[serde(rename = "requestInputTokens", skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    63→        request_input_tokens: Option<u32>,
    64→        /// Output tokens produced in the final LLM request for this turn.
    65→        #[serde(
    66→            rename = "requestOutputTokens",
    67→            skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none"
    68→        )]
    69→        request_output_tokens: Option<u32>,
    70→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    71→        tool_calls: Option<Vec<PersistedToolCall>>,
    72→        /// Optional provider reasoning/planning text (not final answer text).
    73→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    74→        reasoning: Option<String>,
    75→        /// Raw provider API payload captured during streaming for debugging.
    76→        #[serde(rename = "llmApiResponse", skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    77→        llm_api_response: Option<serde_json::Value>,
    78→        /// Relative media path for TTS audio (e.g. "media/main/run_abc.ogg").
    79→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    80→        audio: Option<String>,
    81→        /// Sequence number matching the user message this responds to.
    82→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    83→        seq: Option<u64>,
    84→        /// Agent run ID linking this response to its parent user message.
    85→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    86→        run_id: Option<String>,
    87→    },
    88→    Tool {
    89→        tool_call_id: String,
    90→        content: String,
    91→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    92→        created_at: Option<u64>,
    93→    },
    94→    /// Tool execution result with structured output (stdout, stderr, exit_code).
    95→    ///
    96→    /// Persisted alongside user/assistant messages so that the UI can
    97→    /// reconstruct exec cards when a session is reloaded.
    98→    #[serde(rename = "tool_result")]
    99→    ToolResult {
   100→        tool_call_id: String,
   101→        tool_name: String,
   102→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   103→        arguments: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   104→        success: bool,
   105→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   106→        result: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   107→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   108→        error: Option<String>,
   109→        /// Provider reasoning/thinking text that preceded this tool call.
   110→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   111→        reasoning: Option<String>,
   112→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   113→        created_at: Option<u64>,
   114→        /// Agent run ID linking this result to its parent run.
   115→        #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   116→        run_id: Option<String>,
   117→    },
   118→}

   120→/// User message content: plain text or multimodal array.
   121→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   122→#[serde(untagged)]
   123→pub enum MessageContent {
   124→    Text(String),
   125→    Multimodal(Vec<ContentBlock>),
   126→}
   127→
   128→/// A single block in multimodal content.
   129→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   130→#[serde(tag = "type", rename_all = "snake_case")]
   131→pub enum ContentBlock {
   132→    Text { text: String },
   133→    ImageUrl { image_url: ImageUrl },
   134→}

   136→/// Image URL data (for multimodal content).
   137→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]

   142→/// A tool call stored in an assistant message.
   143→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   144→pub struct PersistedToolCall {
   145→    pub id: String,

   151→/// Function details in a tool call.

   156→}

   158→impl PersistedMessage {
   159→    /// Create a user message with plain text content.
   160→    pub fn user(text: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
   161→        Self::User {
   162→            content: MessageContent::Text(text.into()),
   163→            created_at: Some(now_ms()),
   164→            audio: None,
   165→            channel: None,
   166→            seq: None,
   167→            run_id: None,
   168→        }
   169→    }
   170→
   171→    /// Create a user message with plain text and channel metadata.
   172→    pub fn user_with_channel(text: impl Into<String>, channel: serde_json::Value) -> Self {
   173→        Self::User {
   174→            content: MessageContent::Text(text.into()),
   175→            created_at: Some(now_ms()),
   176→            audio: None,
   177→            channel: Some(channel),
   178→            seq: None,
   179→            run_id: None,
   180→        }
   181→    }
   182→
   183→    /// Create a user message with multimodal content.
   184→    pub fn user_multimodal(blocks: Vec<ContentBlock>) -> Self {
   185→        Self::User {
   186→            content: MessageContent::Multimodal(blocks),
   187→            created_at: Some(now_ms()),
   188→            audio: None,
   189→            channel: None,
   190→            seq: None,
   191→            run_id: None,
   192→        }
   193→    }
   194→
   195→    /// Create a user message with multimodal content and channel metadata.
   196→    pub fn user_multimodal_with_channel(
   197→        blocks: Vec<ContentBlock>,
   198→        channel: serde_json::Value,
   199→    ) -> Self {
   200→        Self::User {
   201→            content: MessageContent::Multimodal(blocks),
   202→            created_at: Some(now_ms()),
   203→            audio: None,
   204→            channel: Some(channel),
   205→            seq: None,
   206→            run_id: None,
   207→        }
   208→    }
   209→
   210→    /// Create an assistant message with token usage and model info.
   211→    pub fn assistant(
   212→        text: impl Into<String>,
   213→        model: impl Into<String>,
   214→        provider: impl Into<String>,
   215→        input_tokens: u32,
   216→        output_tokens: u32,
   217→        audio: Option<String>,
   218→    ) -> Self {
   219→        Self::Assistant {
   220→            content: text.into(),
   221→            created_at: Some(now_ms()),
   222→            model: Some(model.into()),
   223→            provider: Some(provider.into()),
   224→            input_tokens: Some(input_tokens),
   225→            output_tokens: Some(output_tokens),
   226→            duration_ms: None,
   227→            request_input_tokens: Some(input_tokens),
   228→            request_output_tokens: Some(output_tokens),
   229→            tool_calls: None,
   230→            reasoning: None,
   231→            llm_api_response: None,
   232→            audio,
   233→            seq: None,
   234→            run_id: None,
   235→        }
   236→    }
   237→
   238→    /// Create a system message (e.g., for error display).
   239→    pub fn system(text: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
   240→        Self::System {
   241→            content: text.into(),
   242→            created_at: Some(now_ms()),
   243→        }
   244→    }
   245→
   246→    /// Create a notice message shown in UI but skipped from model context.
   247→    pub fn notice(text: impl Into<String>) -> Self {

   253→
   254→    /// Create a tool result message.
   255→    pub fn tool(tool_call_id: impl Into<String>, content: impl Into<String>) -> Self {

   279→            reasoning: None,

   285→    /// Create a tool execution result message with reasoning text.
   286→    pub fn tool_result_with_reasoning(
   287→        tool_call_id: impl Into<String>,

   291→        result: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   292→        error: Option<String>,
   293→        reasoning: Option<String>,

   296→            tool_call_id: tool_call_id.into(),

   298→            arguments,
   299→            success,
   300→            result,
   301→            error,

   303→            created_at: Some(now_ms()),

   307→
   308→    /// Create a tool result message with a run ID linking it to its agent run.
   309→    pub fn tool_result_with_run_id(
   310→        tool_call_id: impl Into<String>,
   311→        tool_name: impl Into<String>,
   312→        arguments: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   313→        success: bool,
   314→        result: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   315→        error: Option<String>,
   316→        run_id: impl Into<String>,
   317→    ) -> Self {
   318→        Self::ToolResult {
   319→            tool_call_id: tool_call_id.into(),
   320→            tool_name: tool_name.into(),
   321→            arguments,
   322→            success,
   323→            result,
   324→            error,
   325→            reasoning: None,
   326→            created_at: Some(now_ms()),
   327→            run_id: Some(run_id.into()),
   328→        }
   329→    }
   330→
   331→    /// Convert to JSON value for storage.
   332→    ///
   333→    /// This cannot fail because `PersistedMessage` only contains types with
   334→    /// infallible serialization (strings, numbers, booleans, vecs, options).
   335→    #[allow(clippy::expect_used)]
   336→    pub fn to_value(&self) -> serde_json::Value {
   337→        serde_json::to_value(self).expect("PersistedMessage serialization cannot fail")
   338→    }
   339→}

   347→    /// Create an image URL content block from base64 data.
   348→    pub fn image_base64(media_type: &str, data: &str) -> Self {
   349→        Self::ImageUrl {

   357→fn now_ms() -> u64 {
   358→    std::time::SystemTime::now()
   359→        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
   360→        .unwrap_or_default()
   361→        .as_millis() as u64
   362→}

Now I have the complete picture. Let me enter plan mode.

Here's the analysis and plan for fixing **GitHub issue #501: Manual context compaction breaks chat**.

## Root Cause

Both compaction sites (`compact()` at line 4391 and `compact_session()` at line 6907 in `crates/chat/src/lib.rs`) store the summary as `PersistedMessage::Assistant`. After compaction, the session history becomes:

```
system (prompt) → assistant (summary) → system (datetime) → user (new message)
```

Strict LLM servers like llama.cpp require every `assistant` message to be preceded by a `user` message. The orphan `assistant` summary violates this, causing: *"No user query found in messages."*

## Proposed Fix

**Change the compacted summary from `PersistedMessage::Assistant` to `PersistedMessage::System`.**

A system message is semantically correct — the summary is context, not an LLM response. After the fix, the message sequence becomes:

```
system (prompt) → system (summary) → system (datetime) → user (new message)
```

This is valid for all providers. Two locations need the same change:

1. **`crates/chat/src/lib.rs:4391`** — `compact()` method (manual `/compact` command)
2. **`crates/chat/src/lib.rs:6907`** — `compact_session()` function (auto-compact on context overflow)

Change `PersistedMessage::Assistant { ... }` → `PersistedMessage::System { content, created_at }` at both sites.

## Regression Test

Add a test that:
1. Seeds a session with a few user/assistant message pairs
2. Calls compaction
3. Asserts the compacted message has `"role": "system"` (not `"assistant"`)
4. Simulates a follow-up by converting the compacted history via `values_to_chat_messages()` and verifying no `ChatMessage::Assistant` appears before the first `ChatMessage::User`

This directly prevents the regression — if anyone changes the role back, the test catches it.

All 151 tests pass. Here's a summary of the changes:

## Changes Made

**Root cause:** Both compaction paths stored the conversation summary as `PersistedMessage::Assistant`, creating an orphan assistant message with no preceding user message. Strict LLM servers (llama.cpp with Qwen template) reject this.

**Fix:** Changed both compaction sites to use `PersistedMessage::System` instead:

1. **`crates/chat/src/lib.rs:4391`** — `compact()` method (manual `/compact`)
2. **`crates/chat/src/lib.rs:6907`** — `compact_session()` function (auto-compact on overflow)
3. **`crates/chat/src/lib.rs:10022`** — Updated existing test assertion from `"assistant"` to `"system"`
4. **`crates/chat/src/lib.rs:10047`** — New regression test `compact_session_produces_system_role` that:
   - Seeds a session, runs compaction, asserts the result has `role: "system"`
   - Converts via `values_to_chat_messages()` and verifies no `Assistant` appears before the first `User`
